# Seed protocol + graph-component records

## Context

Spec-014 ships the `/p` slash command. Without seed protocols, `/p` returns
an empty dropdown on first launch — bad UX. This spec seeds the record store
with a minimal set of real, schema-valid protocols and graph-components that
exercise both plate and tube workflows.

At least one protocol MUST reference a tube rack (from spec-013) so we
prove end-to-end that the system isn't plate-only.

## Requirements

1. **Read the schemas first:**
   - `schema/workflow/protocol.schema.yaml` — required fields: `kind`,
     `recordId`, `title`, `steps`. Also: `shortSlug`, `description`,
     `version`, `state`, `parameters`, `roles` (with `labwareRoles`,
     `materialRoles`), `steps` array, optional `producedArtifacts`.
   - `schema/workflow/graph-component.schema.yaml` — required fields.
   - Read `schema/workflow/well-selector.schema.yaml` (or search for
     `WellSelector` in the schema tree) to understand how steps reference
     wells in the labware.

2. **Create `records/seed/protocols/` directory** and write five protocol
   records. Each file is named after its recordId (no underscores, use
   hyphens).

   | filename | recordId | title | labwareRole(s) | materialRole(s) | brief step summary |
   |---|---|---|---|---|---|
   | `prt-seed-pbs-wash.yaml` | `prt-seed-pbs-wash` | Generic PBS Wash | `plate: 96-well` | `wash_buffer: PBS` | (1) add 200 µL PBS to all wells; (2) incubate 2 min; (3) aspirate |
   | `prt-seed-serial-dilution-96.yaml` | `prt-seed-serial-dilution-96` | 10-Point Serial Dilution (96-Well Plate) | `source_plate: 96-well`, `dest_plate: 96-well` | `stock: generic` | (1) add 180 µL diluent to B1-B10; (2) transfer 20 µL stock to B1; (3) serial transfer 20 µL across B1→B10 |
   | `prt-seed-standard-curve-tubes.yaml` | `prt-seed-standard-curve-tubes` | 4-Point Standard Curve (1.5 mL Tubes) | `tube_rack: 50-position 1.5 mL` | `standard_stock: generic` | (1) add 900 µL buffer to positions A1-A4; (2) add 100 µL stock to A1; (3) serial dilute 100 µL A1→A2→A3→A4 — **this is the tube-rack protocol** |
   | `prt-seed-buffer-prep-50ml.yaml` | `prt-seed-buffer-prep-50ml` | Buffer Preparation in 50 mL Conical | `tube_rack: mixed-4x50-6x15` | `base_buffer: generic`, `additive: generic` | (1) add 40 mL base buffer to rack well A4 (50 mL position); (2) add 1 mL additive; (3) mix by inversion 10 cycles — **uses mixed rack** |
   | `prt-seed-incubate-plate.yaml` | `prt-seed-incubate-plate` | Generic Plate Incubation | `plate: 96-well` | (none — no material additions) | (1) incubate at 37°C for 30 minutes |

3. **Each protocol YAML must have:**

   ```yaml
   $schema: https://computable-lab.com/schema/computable-lab/protocol.schema.yaml
   kind: protocol
   recordId: <id>
   shortSlug: <same as recordId minus prefix>
   title: "<title>"
   description: "Seed protocol shipped with computable-lab for out-of-box usage."
   version: "1.0.0"
   state: effective
   tags: [seed]
   roles:
     labwareRoles:
       - name: <role_name>
         description: <role_description>
         allowedTypes: [<labwareType enum value>]
     materialRoles:
       - name: <role_name>
         description: <role_description>
   parameters: []
   steps:
     - id: step-1
       action: <add_material|transfer|mix|wash|incubate|etc.>
       target:
         labwareRole: <role_name>
         wells:
           kind: explicit
           wells: ["A1", "A2", ...]
       # action-specific fields per schema
   ```

   Defer to the actual schema if field names differ — the schema is the
   source of truth.

4. **For the tube-rack protocols** (`standard-curve-tubes` and
   `buffer-prep-50ml`), use the `allowedTypes` field on the labware role
   to reference the exact enum values from spec-012:
   - `prt-seed-standard-curve-tubes` → `allowedTypes: [tubeset_50x1p5ml]`
   - `prt-seed-buffer-prep-50ml` → `allowedTypes: [tubeset_mixed_4x50ml_6x15ml]`

   In the steps that target the mixed rack, use Opentrons-compatible
   well labels (e.g., `wells: ["A4"]` for the 50 mL position).

5. **Create `records/seed/graph-components/` directory** and write two
   graph-component records:

   | filename | recordId | title | purpose |
   |---|---|---|---|
   | `gc-seed-distribute-to-wells.yaml` | `gc-seed-distribute-to-wells` | Distribute material to wells | Takes a material role and a labware role, distributes N µL to all selected wells |
   | `gc-seed-mix-and-incubate.yaml` | `gc-seed-mix-and-incubate` | Mix and incubate | Mix N cycles, then incubate at T°C for D minutes |

   Each graph-component YAML follows
   `schema/workflow/graph-component.schema.yaml`. Read the schema and
   model them as reusable sub-procedures with parameterized role bindings.

6. **Parse test** at `server/src/seeds/protocol.seed.test.ts`:

   ```ts
   import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
   import { readdirSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';
   import { resolve } from 'node:path';
   import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
   import { load } from 'js-yaml';

   const here = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
   const repoRoot = resolve(here, '..', '..', '..', '..');
   const protocolsDir = resolve(repoRoot, 'records', 'seed', 'protocols');
   const componentsDir = resolve(repoRoot, 'records', 'seed', 'graph-components');

   describe('seed protocol records', () => {
     const files = readdirSync(protocolsDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.yaml'));
     it('has at least 5 protocols', () => expect(files.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5));
     for (const file of files) {
       it(`parses ${file}`, () => {
         const parsed = load(readFileSync(resolve(protocolsDir, file), 'utf8')) as Record<string, unknown>;
         expect(parsed.kind).toBe('protocol');
         expect(parsed.recordId as string).toMatch(/^prt-seed-/);
         expect(typeof parsed.title).toBe('string');
         expect(Array.isArray(parsed.steps)).toBe(true);
         expect((parsed.steps as unknown[]).length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);
       });
     }
   });

   describe('seed graph-component records', () => {
     const files = readdirSync(componentsDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.yaml'));
     it('has at least 2 graph-components', () => expect(files.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2));
     for (const file of files) {
       it(`parses ${file}`, () => {
         const parsed = load(readFileSync(resolve(componentsDir, file), 'utf8')) as Record<string, unknown>;
         expect(parsed.kind).toBe('graph-component');
         expect(parsed.recordId as string).toMatch(/^gc-seed-/);
       });
     }
   });

   describe('at least one seed protocol uses a tube rack', () => {
     it('finds a protocol with allowedTypes including a tubeset type', () => {
       const files = readdirSync(protocolsDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.yaml'));
       let found = false;
       for (const file of files) {
         const raw = readFileSync(resolve(protocolsDir, file), 'utf8');
         if (/tubeset_/.test(raw)) { found = true; break; }
       }
       expect(found).toBe(true);
     });
   });
   ```

## Files Likely Involved

- `records/seed/protocols/prt-seed-pbs-wash.yaml` (new)
- `records/seed/protocols/prt-seed-serial-dilution-96.yaml` (new)
- `records/seed/protocols/prt-seed-standard-curve-tubes.yaml` (new)
- `records/seed/protocols/prt-seed-buffer-prep-50ml.yaml` (new)
- `records/seed/protocols/prt-seed-incubate-plate.yaml` (new)
- `records/seed/graph-components/gc-seed-distribute-to-wells.yaml` (new)
- `records/seed/graph-components/gc-seed-mix-and-incubate.yaml` (new)
- `server/src/seeds/protocol.seed.test.ts` (new)

## Verification

```bash
cd server
ls ../records/seed/protocols/*.yaml | wc -l        # >= 5
ls ../records/seed/graph-components/*.yaml | wc -l # >= 2
npx vitest run src/seeds/protocol.seed.test.ts
npx tsc --noEmit
grep -r "tubeset_" ../records/seed/protocols/ | wc -l    # >= 1
```

Expected: counts match, tests pass, typecheck passes, grep finds at least one tube rack reference.

## Notes

- **Defer to the actual schemas** over this spec's field descriptions. If
  the schema requires different field names, use the schema.
- **Keep steps concrete and minimal.** A protocol with 3-5 steps is
  better than one with 20 — seeds are starting points, not final recipes.
- **Do not run `pnpm test` or `pnpm lint`.** Use only `npx tsc --noEmit`
  and targeted `npx vitest run <file>`.
- **Do not reference records that don't exist** (except by role, not by
  concrete binding — protocols are abstract).

## Non-Negotiable Invariants

- At least one seed protocol references a tube rack via `allowedTypes`.
- Every `recordId` uses the correct prefix.
- Every file validates against its schema.
- All seed protocols live under `records/seed/protocols/` only.

## Dependencies and Tooling

None beyond `js-yaml` (already a server dep).

## Negative Cases

- Must NOT invent schema fields not present in the actual schema files.
- Must NOT create protocols that reference specific material or labware
  record IDs — they reference roles only.
- Must NOT skip the tube rack requirement.
- Must NOT write protocols longer than necessary — these are seed
  examples, not production recipes.

## Repo Commands

- install: pnpm install
- lint: pnpm lint
- test: pnpm test
- build: pnpm build
- command runner: /home/brad/git/agent-workbench/scripts/run-repo-command.sh

## Tool Usage Instructions

# Retrieval Tool

The workbench has pre-indexed the target repository using lexical search. Results are injected into the **Retrieval Highlights** section of this prompt.

## How to read results

Each result line follows this format:
```
- <relative_path>:<start_line>-<end_line> score=<rerank_score> reason=<explanation> symbol=<name>
```

- **score**: higher is more relevant (combines lexical match + reranking boosts)
- **reason**: explains why this chunk ranked highly (e.g., "token overlap + path match", "symbol match")
- **symbol**: the function, class, or heading name in that chunk

## How to use retrieval results

1. **Before editing unfamiliar code**, check retrieval results for the relevant file and line range. Read those lines to understand existing patterns before making changes.
2. **Use file:line references** to navigate directly to the code rather than searching from scratch.
3. **Higher-scored results are more likely relevant** to the current task. If the top results point to a file, that file is likely important.
4. **Symbol names** tell you which function or class is in that chunk — use them to understand the codebase structure.
5. **If retrieval returned no matches**, the task may involve new files or areas not well-covered by the index. Explore the repo directory structure instead.

# Playwright MCP Tool

Playwright MCP provides browser automation for testing web UIs. It is available as an MCP server — you can call its tools directly.

## When to use

- The app is running and you need to verify UI behavior
- You need to test user flows (login, form submission, navigation)
- You want visual verification of rendered output

## When NOT to use

- No app is configured or the app is not running
- The task is backend-only with no UI component

## Available tools

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `browser_navigate` | Navigate to a URL |
| `browser_snapshot` | Get the current page's accessibility snapshot (DOM structure) |
| `browser_click` | Click an element by CSS selector or text |
| `browser_fill` | Fill a form input field |
| `browser_type` | Type text character by character |
| `browser_select_option` | Select from a dropdown |
| `browser_press_key` | Press a keyboard key |
| `browser_take_screenshot` | Capture a screenshot for visual verification |
| `browser_hover` | Hover over an element |
| `browser_wait_for` | Wait for an element or condition |

## Example workflow

1. Find the **App URL** in the **MCP Metadata** section of the prompt (e.g., `http://localhost:5174`)
2. Navigate: `browser_navigate` to that URL
3. Inspect: `browser_snapshot` to see the DOM structure and verify the page rendered
4. Interact: `browser_click` on buttons, `browser_fill` form fields
5. Verify: `browser_snapshot` again to confirm expected state after interaction

## Important

- The Playwright MCP args file listed in MCP Metadata is the launch contract — do not modify it.
- The browser runs headless. Use `browser_snapshot` for DOM inspection rather than relying on visual output.
- If the app healthcheck hasn't passed, Playwright interactions will fail.

## Planner Guidance

# Planner

1. Read the task, repo config, retrieval results, and MCP metadata from the generated loop context.
2. Identify the smallest coherent implementation step that moves the task forward.
3. Choose the minimum file set needed for that step.
4. Decide which repo commands must be run to verify the change.
5. Avoid speculative refactors unless the task requires them.

## Coder Guidance

# Coder

1. Make changes in the target repository only.
2. Prefer the repo-specific commands from the loop context when running checks.
3. Use the retrieval results to confirm file locations and nearby symbols before editing.
4. Keep changes narrow and task-directed.
5. **After making UI changes**: If Playwright MCP is available (status: ready-stdio) and an app is running, you MUST use `browser_navigate` to load the **App URL listed in MCP Metadata above**, then `browser_snapshot` to verify the page renders without errors. Do not skip this step — check the MCP Metadata section for the exact URL.
6. **Before writing API client code**: If OpenAPI MCP is available (spec count > 0), use it to look up the endpoint schema first. Do not guess at request/response shapes.
7. **When stuck on unfamiliar libraries or APIs**: If Exa web search MCP is available, use `web_search_exa` or `get_code_context_exa` to look up documentation and examples.

## Critic Guidance

# Critic

1. Verify the intended behavior with the most relevant available command (tsc, tests, lint).
2. If UI changes were made and Playwright MCP is available, verify the page loads and renders by using `browser_navigate` and `browser_snapshot`.
3. Re-check the task and confirm the edited files actually satisfy each acceptance criterion.
4. Append concrete learnings to the progress log for the next iteration — what was done, what was verified, what's left.
5. If the task cannot be completed safely, leave a precise explanation in the progress log.
6. When ALL criteria are met: output `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` as the absolute last line. Nothing after it.

rack** |\n   | `prt-seed-incubate-plate.yaml` | `prt-seed-incubate-plate` | Generic Plate Incubation | `plate: 96-well` | (none \u2014 no material additions) | (1) incubate at 37\u00b0C for 30 minutes |\n\n3. **Each protocol YAML must have:**\n\n   ```yaml\n   $schema: https://computable-lab.com/schema/computable-lab/protocol.schema.yaml\n   kind: protocol\n   recordId: <id>\n   shortSlug: <same as recordId minus prefix>\n   title: \"<title>\"\n   description: \"Seed protocol shipped with computable-lab for out-of-box usage.\"\n   version: \"1.0.0\"\n   state: effective\n   tags: [seed]\n   roles:\n     labwareRoles:\n       - name: <role_name>\n         description: <role_description>\n         allowedTypes: [<labwareType enum value>]\n     materialRoles:\n       - name: <role_name>\n         description: <role_description>\n   parameters: []\n   steps:\n     - id: step-1\n       action: <add_material|transfer|mix|wash|incubate|etc.>\n       target:\n         labwareRole: <role_name>\n         wells:\n           kind: explicit\n           wells: [\"A1\", \"A2\", ...]\n       # action-specific fields per schema\n   ```\n\n   Defer to the actual schema if field names differ \u2014 the schema is the\n   source of truth.\n\n4. **For the tube-rack protocols** (`standard-curve-tubes` and\n   `buffer-prep-50ml`), use the `allowedTypes` field on the labware role\n   to reference the exact enum values from spec-012:\n   - `prt-seed-standard-curve-tubes` \u2192 `allowedTypes: [tubeset_50x1p5ml]`\n   - `prt-seed-buffer-prep-50ml` \u2192 `allowedTypes: [tubeset_mixed_4x50ml_6x15ml]`\n\n   In the steps that target the mixed rack, use Opentrons-compatible\n   well labels (e.g., `wells: [\"A4\"]` for the 50 mL position).\n\n5. **Create `records/seed/graph-components/` directory** and write two\n   graph-component records:\n\n   | filename | recordId | title | purpose |\n   |---|---|---|---|\n   | `gc-seed-distribute-to-wells.yaml` | `gc-seed-distribute-to-wells` | Distribute material to wells | Takes a material role and a labware role, distributes N \u00b5L to all selected wells |\n   | `gc-seed-mix-and-incubate.yaml` | `gc-seed-mix-and-incubate` | Mix and incubate | Mix N cycles, then incubate at T\u00b0C for D minutes |\n\n   Each graph-component YAML follows\n   `schema/workflow/graph-component.schema.yaml`. Read the schema and\n   model them as reusable sub-procedures with parameterized role bindings.\n\n6. **Parse test** at `server/src/seeds/protocol.seed.test.ts`:\n\n   ```ts\n   import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';\n   import { readdirSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs';\n   import { resolve } from 'node:path';\n   import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';\n   import { load } from 'js-yaml';\n\n   const here = fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);\n   const repoRoot = resolve(here, '..', '..', '..', '..');\n   const protocolsDir = resolve(repoRoot, 'records', 'seed', 'protocols');\n   const componentsDir = resolve(repoRoot, 'records', 'seed', 'graph-components');\n\n   describe('seed protocol records', () => {\n     const files = readdirSync(protocolsDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.yaml'));\n     it('has at least 5 protocols', () => expect(files.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5));\n     for (const file of files) {\n       it(`parses ${file}`, () => {\n         const parsed = load(readFileSync(resolve(protocolsDir, file), 'utf8')) as Record<string, unknown>;\n         expect(parsed.kind).toBe('protocol');\n         expect(parsed.recordId as string).toMatch(/^prt-seed-/);\n         expect(typeof parsed.title).toBe('string');\n         expect(Array.isArray(parsed.steps)).toBe(true);\n         expect((parsed.steps as unknown[]).length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(1);\n       });\n     }\n   });\n\n   describe('seed graph-component records', () => {\n     const files = readdirSync(componentsDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.yaml'));\n     it('has at least 2 graph-components', () => expect(files.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(2));\n     for (const file of files) {\n       it(`parses ${file}`, () => {\n         const parsed = load(readFileSync(resolve(componentsDir, file), 'utf8')) as Record<string, unknown>;\n         expect(parsed.kind).toBe('graph-component');\n         expect(parsed.recordId as string).toMatch(/^gc-seed-/);\n       });\n     }\n   });\n\n   describe('at least one seed protocol uses a tube rack', () => {\n     it('finds a protocol with allowedTypes including a tubeset type', () => {\n       const files = readdirSync(protocolsDir).filter((f) => f.endsWith('.yaml'));\n       let found = false;\n       for (const file of files) {\n         const raw = readFileSync(resolve(protocolsDir, file), 'utf8');\n         if (/tubeset_/.test(raw)) { found = true; break; }\n       }\n       expect(found).toBe(true);\n     });\n   });\n   ```\n\n## Files Likely ## Delivery Rules

1. Focus on one story only.
2. Use retrieval evidence before editing unfamiliar areas.
3. Prefer repo-configured lint, test, and build commands when verifying changes.
4. If you use Playwright or OpenAPI MCP, treat the generated args/config files as the launch contract.
5. **After any UI change, you MUST verify in the browser.** Use `browser_navigate` to load the App URL from MCP Metadata above, then `browser_snapshot` to confirm the page renders. If it shows errors, fix them before marking the story complete.
6. **Tool-usage criteria are enforced.** Acceptance criteria may explicitly require a specific tool call (e.g. *"at least one `browser_snapshot` against the rendered page"*). The critic sees a per-tool count from your trace and will auto-reject if a required tool shows zero calls — tsc passing is not enough. Read every criterion and make sure you have actually used the tools each one demands before emitting the completion promise.
7. When the story is complete, update the progress log file at `/home/brad/git/agent-workbench/state/sessions/computable-lab/2026-04-14-assistant-speedup/specs/spec-015-seed-protocol-records.md` with what you did.
8. If the task is blocked, record the exact blocker and stop cleanly.
9. **CRITICAL — Completion signal**: When and ONLY when ALL acceptance criteria are met, output `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` as the **very last line** of your response. Nothing may follow it — no summary, no explanation, no whitespace. If ralph does not detect this exact tag as your final line, the loop will continue unnecessarily.
